title: balsamorhiza incana var. tomentosa
intro: Balsamorhiza incana var. tomentosa, also known as woolly balsamroot, is a perennial herb belonging to the sunflower family (Asteraceae) native to the western United States and Canada. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids. The health benefits of woolly balsamroot include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation and pain, and exhibit antioxidant properties, making it a valuable herb for treating various health conditions. Woolly balsamroot is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and salves, which are used to treat skin issues, wounds, and digestive problems.
